purpose: Convert repeated chat-level troubleshooting, improvement, and preference patterns into durable MetaBlooms OS behavior so future work starts from learned methods rather than requiring Robert to re-explain the process.

principles.name: artifact_backed_learning_only
principles.rule: A workflow lesson is not considered learned until represented by artifacts such as policies, schemas, validators, fixtures, ledgers, routing metadata, templates, or executable resolvers. Chat-only memory is insufficient.

principles.name: generalize_after_repair
principles.rule: When a workflow repairs a failure, the system must classify the failure class, identify the reusable method improvement, and decide whether to instantiate a persistent prevention or routing artifact.

principles.name: sandbox_first_plus_enhancement_lane
principles.rule: For ordinary Robert workflows, the ChatGPT sandbox path is primary. PC/PowerShell/CI paths may be generated as enhancement lanes but must not block sandbox progress unless the artifact is explicitly production-critical.

principles.name: automatic_future_invocation
principles.rule: If a learned method applies to a future task class, the relevant cartridge, gate, resolver, or checklist must be auto-invoked by routing metadata rather than waiting for Robert to name it again.

principles.name: honesty_tier_labels
principles.rule: When capability is partial, the OS must label the evidence tier honestly, such as sandbox_render_proxy, static_only_preflight, or true_browser_screenshot, instead of claiming stronger proof.

principles.name: minimum_valid_artifact_set
principles.rule: A generalized lesson should normally include a policy or contract, schema if structured data is involved, fixtures/evals, validator or resolver when executable, reliability or failure ledger entry, receipt, handoff, and export-prep binding when it affects future outputs.
